author: OpenTikZ contributors
description: FlashAttention, three-panel homage after Dao et al., 2022. Left: the GPU memory hierarchy as a banded triangle (SRAM 19 TB/s / HBM 1.5 TB/s / DRAM 12.8 GB/s) showing the bandwidth-vs-size trade-off. Center: the IO-aware dataflow — Q, K^T and V tiles streamed from HBM, copied block-by-block into SRAM (outer loop over K/V, inner loop over Q), where the QK^T block and softmax are fused before sm(QK^T)V is written back to HBM. Right: the GPT-2 attention runtime, a stacked PyTorch bar (Matmul/Mask/Softmax/Dropout/Matmul) versus FlashAttention's single fused kernel.
name: FlashAttention (IO-aware attention)

author: OpenTikZ contributors
description: Block-level GAN overview: a low-dimensional latent bar feeds the generator G (expanding trapezoid) producing generated fake images, while a high-dimensional sample-space stack supplies real images; both feed the discriminator D (compressing trapezoid) that outputs a real/fake decision. Parametric sizes, colors, and labels.
name: Generative Adversarial Network (GAN)

author: OpenTikZ contributors
description: Low-rank adaptation (LoRA), two-panel homage after Hu et al., 2021. During training: a frozen pretrained weight W feeds Wx in parallel with a trainable low-rank update B·A (rank-r bottleneck, A=N(0,sigma^2), B=0) feeding BAx, summed to h. After training: the adapter is folded into a single merged weight matrix W_merged, shown as a bar–square–bar stack. Center column gives h = Wx + BAx = (W + BA)x.
name: LoRA (low-rank adaptation)

author: Daniel Huang
description: LLM agent tool-use stack with brand logos: user to an LLM core to MCP servers fanning out to swappable tool cards.

invariants: Structural colors only via the five palette names; brand marks keep their official brand\* colors (exception documented in icons/brands/README.md).
Node names user/llm/mcp/t1..t3 are stable API — never rename them.
svg.path ignores TikZ unit-vector options (x=, y=): position/size logo pics only via shift/scale.
The file must stay standalone-compilable.
edit_contract.node_naming: user, llm, mcp; tool cards t1..t3 top-to-bottom; logos are pics named pic<brand>

operations.summary: Change any card or edge text via the label \def parameters only.
Replace a tool card's logo: swap the \pic call's pic<brand> name and inline the new brand's \definecolor + pic definition from icons/brands/<brand>/.
Duplicate the t3 block as t4 with yshift=-2*(\cardh+\toolgap), add a flow edge from mcp to t4.west, and a \tooldlabel parameter.
Scale all cards via \cardw/\cardh; keep pic scale ~0.26-0.3 so logos stay inside cards.
Change card fills/arrows via the five palette names (tints allowed); never touch the brand\* colors.

parameters.meaning: label of the top tool card (t1)
parameters.name: \toolalabel

parameters.meaning: label of the middle tool card (t2)
parameters.name: \toolblabel

parameters.meaning: label of the bottom tool card (t3)
parameters.name: \toolclabel

name: Agent Tool-Use Stack (MCP)

author: OpenTikZ contributors
description: Editable data-parallel training figure composing the dataset, gpu, and server icons: a training-data source shards batches to GPU workers that exchange gradients and weights with a parameter server. Parametric spacing and labels.

invariants: the data/gradient/weight flows must stay in distinct palette colors (gray data, purple gradients, teal dashed weights)
every worker added to the layout must also appear in the gradient/weight sync \foreach list
colors only via the five palette names (tints/shades allowed); never inline hex or stock colors
edit_contract.node_naming: fixed semantic names: (data) source; GPU workers (w1)(w2)(w3) top-to-bottom; (ps) parameter server. New workers continue the w<n> sequence and are positioned at (\colsep, k*\rowsep)

operations.summary: edit the matching label macro (\datalabel, \worker*label, \pslabel, \titlelabel)
declare \node[icon] (w4) at (\colsep, k*\rowsep) {\gpupic} plus its slbl label, then add it to the sync \foreach list and a data edge from (data.east)
edit \colsep (stage gap) or \rowsep (worker gap); the server and title positions are derived from \colsep/\rowsep
replace a node body picture macro (\datasetpic, \gpupic, \serverpic) with another icon sub-picture adapted from icons/
change the palette name in the data/grad/wts styles or an icon macro; keep distinct flows (data/gradient/weight) in distinct colors; never inline hex

parameters.meaning: horizontal gap between the three stages (data -> workers -> server)
parameters.name: \colsep

parameters.meaning: vertical gap between adjacent GPU workers
parameters.name: \rowsep

name: Distributed data-parallel training

author: OpenTikZ contributors
description: Block-level encoder-decoder 'hourglass': input block, encoder trapezoid narrowing to a latent bottleneck z, decoder trapezoid widening to an output block. Parametric sizes and labels.

invariants: preserve the node names (input) (enc) (z) (dec) (output)
keep each trapezoid's inner-edge height equal to \latentH so encoder/decoder stay flush with z
colors only via the five palette names (tints/shades allowed); never inline hex or stock colors
edit_contract.node_naming: fixed semantic node names: (input) (enc) (z) (dec) (output); (enc)/(dec) are the centre text nodes, not the trapezoid paths

operations.summary: edit the matching label macro; no layout change needed
change the palette name in the encoder/decoder \filldraw or the latentblk style; never a hex/stock color
\blockH/\blockW for I/O blocks, \trapW for trapezoid length, \latentH/\latentW for the bottleneck, \gap for spacing
place a palette-colored block with positioning above (z) that (enc) feeds and that feeds (dec); connect with flow arrows; keep (enc)/(z)/(dec) intact
insert another running edge pair after the relevant part, push later edges along by its width + \gap, draw the node and an extra flow arrow; keep every part centred on y=0

parameters.meaning: height of the input/output blocks; also each trapezoid's wide (outer) edge
parameters.name: \blockH

parameters.meaning: height of the bottleneck z; also each trapezoid's narrow (inner) edge, so shapes meet z flush
parameters.name: \latentH

parameters.meaning: horizontal gap between parts (also the arrow length)
parameters.name: \gap

parameters.meaning: bottleneck label drawn inside z (keep short, e.g. $z$)
parameters.name: \latentlabel

name: Encoder-decoder (bottleneck)

author: OpenTikZ contributors
description: Standard flowchart: terminal pills (start/end), process rectangles, a decision diamond with Yes/No branches, and a retry loop. Parametric spacing and labels.

invariants: preserve the node names (start) (read) (check) (proc) (end) (error) and the per-role styles terminal/process/decision
label every decision's outgoing edges (Yes/No or equivalent)
colors only via the five palette names (tints/shades allowed); never inline hex or stock colors
edit_contract.node_naming: fixed semantic node names: (start) (read) (check) (proc) (end) (error); give any new node a clear semantic name

operations.summary: edit the matching label macro
insert a process node below=of the previous one and re-point the surrounding flow arrows
add a decision node and route its two outgoing flow arrows, labelling each branch
swap a node's style: process<->decision<->terminal (give a decision a ? label)
use a trapezium (slanted box) for an I/O step; shapes.geometric is already loaded
change the palette name in the relevant style; never a hex/stock color

parameters.meaning: vertical gap between steps (tikz node distance)
parameters.name: \rowsep

name: Flowchart (decision + loop)

author: OpenTikZ contributors
description: Editable deployment figure composing the cloud, server, gpu, and database icons: client requests flow over the internet through a load balancer to GPU-backed inference nodes that read from a feature store, with a dashed inference-cluster boundary. Parametric spacing and labels.

invariants: the request path (req, solid) and feature reads (read, teal dashed) must stay visually distinct
the (cluster) boundary node must be drawn in the on-background-layer scope after the nodes it fits, so it sits behind them
any inference node inside the boundary must be added to the (cluster) fit=(...) list
colors only via the five palette names (tints/shades allowed); never inline hex or stock colors
edit_contract.node_naming: fixed semantic names along the request path: (client) (cloud) (lb); inference nodes (node1)(node2) with their labels (n1lab)(n2lab); (db) feature store; (cluster) dashed boundary box. New nodes get role-based names and join the (cluster) fit list if inside the boundary

operations.summary: edit the matching label macro (\clientlabel, \lblabel, \node*label, \storelabel, \titlelabel)

declare \node[nodebox] (node3) at (3*\colsep, y) {\serverpic\hspace{4pt}\gpupic} plus its slbl label, route a req edge from (lb.east) and a read edge to (db.west), then add the node and its label to the (cluster) fit list
edit \colsep (stage gap) or \rowsep (node offset); node and store x-positions are multiples of \colsep, title position is derived from \colsep/\rowsep
replace a node body picture macro (\cloudpic, \serverpic, \gpupic, \dbpic) with another icon sub-picture adapted from icons/
change the palette name in the req/read styles or an icon macro; keep request vs feature-read flows in distinct colors; never inline hex

parameters.meaning: horizontal gap between request-path stages (client -> cloud -> lb -> nodes -> store)
parameters.name: \colsep

parameters.meaning: vertical offset of the two stacked inference nodes from the centerline
parameters.name: \rowsep

name: Model inference serving

author: Daniel Huang
description: LLM inference serving stack with brand logos: users to a vLLM engine on a GPU cluster, serving swappable model cards.

invariants: Structural colors only via the five palette names; brand marks keep their official brand\* colors (exception documented in icons/brands/README.md).
Node names users/engine/gpus/m1..m3 are stable API — never rename them.
svg.path ignores TikZ unit-vector options (x=, y=): position/size logo pics only via shift/scale.
The file must stay standalone-compilable.
edit_contract.node_naming: users, engine, gpus; model cards m1..m3 top-to-bottom; logos are pics named pic<brand>

operations.summary: Change any card or edge text via the label \def parameters only.
Replace a model card's logo: swap the \pic call's pic<brand> name and inline the new brand's \definecolor + pic definition from icons/brands/<brand>/.
Duplicate the m3 block as m4 with yshift=-2*(\cardh+\modelgap), add a flow edge from m4.west to engine.south east side, and a \modeldlabel parameter.
Scale all cards via \cardw/\cardh; keep pic scale ~0.28-0.3 so logos stay inside cards.
Change card fills/arrows via the five palette names (tints allowed); never touch the brand\* colors.

parameters.meaning: label of the top model card (m1)
parameters.name: \modelalabel

parameters.meaning: label of the middle model card (m2)
parameters.name: \modelblabel

parameters.meaning: label of the bottom model card (m3)
parameters.name: \modelclabel

name: LLM Serving Stack

author: OpenTikZ contributors
description: Parametric fully-connected feed-forward neural network. Layer count and neurons-per-layer are set by a single list at the top of the file.

invariants: \layersizes and \layerlabels stay equal length
preserve the L<layer>-<neuron> node-naming scheme
do not remove the \pgfdeclarelayer{edges} / \pgfsetlayers lines or edges draw over the neurons

operations.summary: append a count to \layersizes AND a label to \layerlabels (keep equal length); insert mid-network by adding at the same position in both lists
delete one entry from each list at the same position
edit one entry in \layersizes only (the label list is unchanged)
change the palette color name used by the neuron style (and otgray in edge/layerlabel for tints); never a hex or stock color
\neuronsize for node size, \neuronsep (vertical) and \layersep (horizontal) for spacing

parameters.meaning: neuron count per layer; the number of entries is the number of layers (layer 1 is leftmost)
parameters.name: \layersizes

parameters.meaning: vertical distance between neurons within a layer
parameters.name: \neuronsep

name: Feed-forward neural network

author: OpenTikZ contributors
description: The classic ResNet residual learning building block (He et al.): a stack of weight layers forming F(x), an identity skip connection that re-enters at a summation node, and a final activation. The number of weight layers is driven by a single list at the top of the file.

invariants: \branchlabels has at least one entry
preserve the wl<i> / xlabel / sum / out node-naming scheme
keep the identity skip connection running from the input line into the sum node (the residual shortcut is the point of the figure)
edit_contract.node_naming: wl<i> for weight-layer boxes (1-based, top to bottom, e.g. wl1, wl2); xlabel for the input; sum for the addition node; out for the output coordinate

operations.summary: append a label to \branchlabels (the relu between layers and the wl<i> naming follow automatically); insert mid-branch by adding at the desired position in the list
delete one entry from \branchlabels (keep at least one)
change \actlabel (e.g. relu -> GELU); it applies to every inter-layer arrow and the post-sum arrow
change the palette color name used by the wlayer style (e.g. otblue -> otteal) and/or otgray in flow/skip/addop; never a hex or stock color
\blockw and \blockh size the boxes; \blocksep sets vertical spacing; \skipbow controls how far the identity path bows right

parameters.meaning: labels of the weight-layer boxes in the residual branch, top to bottom; the number of entries is the number of stacked layers
parameters.name: \branchlabels

parameters.meaning: activation label shown between consecutive weight layers and after the summation
parameters.name: \actlabel

parameters.meaning: side label naming the residual branch
parameters.name: \residuallabel

parameters.meaning: side label at the summation node
parameters.name: \outputlabel

parameters.meaning: upper label on the identity skip path
parameters.name: \skiplabel

parameters.meaning: lower label on the identity skip path
parameters.name: \identitylabel

parameters.meaning: how far right the identity skip connection bows out
parameters.name: \skipbow

name: ResNet residual block

author: OpenTikZ contributors
description: General system architecture diagram: external client, process boxes (gateway/services), datastore cylinders (database/cache), branching connections, and a dashed subsystem group box. Multiple component types, parametric spacing.

invariants: the group node must come after the nodes it fits, inside the on-background-layer scope, so it sits behind them
preserve semantic node names; give new components clear role-based names
colors only via the five palette names (tints/shades allowed); never inline hex or stock colors
edit_contract.node_naming: fixed semantic node names: (client) (gateway) (svc1) (svc2) (db) (cache) (backend); give new components clear role-based names

operations.summary: edit the matching label macro
declare a node with its role style, positioned relative to an existing node, then connect with link; add it to the group fit list if it belongs to the subsystem
swap a node's style to change its role: process<->store<->external
add \draw[link] (a) -- (b); use <-> for two-way, -|/|- for orthogonal routing
adjust the group inner sep (padding) or change which nodes are in its fit=(...) list
change the palette name in the relevant style; keep distinct roles in distinct colors; never a hex/stock color

name: System block diagram
